perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, an "Impression" is defined as any link to the NetGrocer Site, whether graphic, text or any combination of graphic and text, served by Excite to a user as part of an HTML page, part of the Excite's Internet chat service or part of search results displayed in "Excite Shopping Service powered by Jango".
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, an "Impression" is defined as any request to the NetGrocer Site, whether successful or not, and may or may not include a graphic, text or any combination of graphic and text. This includes attempts to access the site even if the user does not fully load the page or leaves before the site is fully displayed. It also excludes access from the Excite's Internet chat service or part of search results displayed in "Excite Shopping Service powered by Jango".
perturbation.explanation: This change to the definition of 'Impression' creates an in-text contradiction. The original definition clearly states an 'Impression' is a *served* link. The modified definition broadens this to include *any request*, regardless of whether the link is actually served or the page fully loaded. This introduces uncertainty in calculating the number of impressions, as Excite could count incomplete or abandoned requests, which is contradicting the last part of the definition, where the impressions will only come from HTML pages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Excite will use reasonable commercial efforts to deliver [*] "Impressions" of the promotional placements and advertising banners described in Sections 1 (a) - (d) in the first year of the term of the Agreement and to deliver [*] "Impressions" of the promotional placements and advertising banners described in Sections 1 (a) - (d) in the second year of the term of the Agreement. For the purposes of this Agreement, an "Impression" is defined as any request to the NetGrocer Site, whether successful or not, and may or may not include a graphic, text or any combination of graphic and text. This includes attempts to access the site even if the user does not fully load the page or leaves before the site is fully displayed. It also excludes access from the Excite's Internet chat service or part of search results displayed in "Excite Shopping Service powered by Jango".
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Impression' introduces a contradiction because it broadens the scope to include incomplete requests that may or may not load fully, while also excluding impressions from certain sources (Excite's Internet chat service). The contradicted text, which includes the changed definition, refers to 'Impressions' of promotional placements, and by broadening the definition and excluding certain sources the total number of impressions will be different, thus creating a direct inconsistency regarding the amount of 'Impressions' Excite is required to deliver. This creates uncertainty about how to calculate the impressions and contradicts the obligation to deliver a certain number of impressions.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, a "Click-through" occurs when a user activates the link to the NetGrocer Site (the address or addresses of which are provided by NetGrocer for such Impression) contained in an Impression and (i) the activation of the link to the NetGrocer Site is recorded by Excite's servers or (ii) in the case of "Try These First" links only, until Excite has the technical capability to count the activation of "Try These First" links to the NetGrocer Site, the user is referred to the NetGrocer Site through the activation of a "Try These First' link and the referral is recorded by NetGrocer's servers.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, a "Click-through" occurs when a user merely hovers their mouse cursor over a link to the NetGrocer Site for more than one second. The registration of this action, which qualifies as a "Click-through", can be recorded by either Excite's or NetGrocer's servers, using either company's preferred method, regardless of the original intent behind establishing measurement practices.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Click-through' requires *activation* of the link. The modified definition changes this to simply *hovering* the mouse cursor. This creates a contradiction because hovering is not the same as clicking, and the revised definition drastically inflates the number of actions counted as a 'Click-through'. This contradicts the commercial reasonableness of payment terms, since Click-through rates were calculated with the original definition of an actual click, not a hover, thus contradicting Section 5 which outlines the payment schedule based on impressions, which will be affected by this modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee and sponsorship and advertising fees, NetGrocer will pay Excite [*] for each Click-through on the promotional placements and advertising banners described in Section 1(a) - (d) occurring during that year in excess of [*] during the first year of the term of the Agreement. NetGrocer will make these Click-through payments (if any) to Excite within thirty (30) days of Excite's monthly report and invoice reflecting Click-throughs during the first year of the term of the Agreement in excess of [*]
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Click-through' from requiring activation to merely hovering the mouse. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies NetGrocer will pay Excite for each *Click-through*. Because the definition of clickthrough is now hovering, the amount of payments that NetGrocer must make is likely to increase, contradicting the original intent behind the payment schedule, creating a direct and impactful conflict that would cause confusion and potentially legal issues.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that disputes between the parties arising from or concerning in any manner the subject matter of this Agreement, other than disputes arising from or concerning trademarks, service marks or trade names and/or confidentiality, cannot be resolved through good faith negotiation and mediation, the parties will refer the dispute(s) to the American Arbitration Association for resolution through binding arbitration by a single arbitrator pursuant to the American Arbitration Association's rules applicable to commercial disputes.
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes arising from or concerning in any manner the subject matter of this Agreement, will be resolved in the courts of California, specifically in Redwood City, and neither good faith negotiation, mediation, or arbitration shall be employed. Each party waives all rights to trial by jury. The losing party shall pay all legal expenses incurred by the prevailing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original dispute resolution clause mandates negotiation, mediation, and then binding arbitration through the American Arbitration Association. The modified version eliminates all of these steps and requires all disputes to be resolved in California courts, with no opportunity for alternative dispute resolution. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the process for resolving disputes and which mechanisms need to be undertaken, thus contradicting section 13(b) which refers to trademark disputes, which are completely being overridden with this modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of disputes between the parties arising from or concerning in any manner the subject matter of this Agreement, other than disputes arising from or concerning trademarks, service marks or trade names and/or confidentiality, the parties will first attempt to resolve the dispute(s) through good faith negotiation. In the event that the dispute(s) cannot be resolved through good faith negotiation, the parties will refer the dispute(s) to a mutually acceptable mediator for hearing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for good faith negotiation and mediation before arbitration, and instead mandates all disputes be resolved in California courts. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that disputes should first be attempted to be resolved through good faith negotiation and then mediation. This satisfies the definition of a structural flaw, where the modified text creates conflicting obligations with the contradicted section.
